title: Act 25 of 2025 Architects Registration Amendment
text:
1
No. 25 of 2025. AArrcchhiitteeccttss RReeggiissttrraattiioonn ((AAmmeennddmmeenntt)) AAcctt,, 22002255. – 25.Saint Christopher
and Nevis.
I assent,
LS
MARCELLA ALTHEA LIBURD
Governor-General
7th November, 2025.
SAINT CHRISTOPHER AND NEVIS
No. 25 of 2025
AN ACT to amend the Architects Registration Act, No. 16 of 2010 and for related
matters.
[Published 7th November 2025, Extra-Ordinary Gazette No. 60 of 2025.]
BE IT ENACTED by the King’s Most Excellent Majesty, by and with the
advice and consent of the National Assembly of Saint Christopher and Nevis, and by the
authority of the same as follows:
1. Short title.
This Act may be cited as the Architects Registration (Amendment) Act, 2025.
2. Interpretation.
In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires
“Act” means the Architects Registration Act, No. 16 of 2010.
3. Amendment of Section 3.
The Act is amended in section 3 by inserting immediately after subsection (2), the
following new subsection (3) as follows
“ (3) The Minister may, prior to appointing the initial members of the Board, consult
with the St. Kitts-Nevis Institute ofArchitects.”.
4. Amendment of section 8.
Section 8 of the Act is amended by inserting immediately after subsection (4), new
subsections (5), (6), (7), (8) and (9) as follows
“ (5) An architect who desires to carry on the practice of architecture in any year
shall apply, by the end of January of each year and in the prescribed form, to the
Registrar for a certificate of authorisation.

22
Architects Registration (Amendment) Act, 2025 – 25.
(6) Subject to the provisions of this section, the Registrar shall, upon payment of
the prescribed fee and the production of the prescribed document, promptly issue to
an architect a certificate of authorisation in the form prescribed.
(7) A certificate of authorisation shall be valid for one year from the date of issue
and shall expire on the anniversary of its issue.
(8) The Registrar shall cause to be published in the Gazette and at least one
newspaper of general circulation in Saint Christopher and Nevis in February of every
year, a list of persons who have in that year been issued with a certificate of authorisation.
(9) A copy of the Gazette and the newspaper referred to in subsection (8) shall be
prima facie evidence in any Court of the fact of the registration of the name of an
architect and that he or she is the holder of a valid certificate of authorisation.”.
5. Amendment of section 9.
Section 9 of the Act is amended by replacing paragraph (c) as follows
“(c)he or she has had not less than ten (10) years of practical experience in
architecture where five of those ten years was acquired in Saint Christopher
and Nevis and he or she can prove his or her proficiency in the field of
architecture to the satisfaction of the Board in accordance with the provisions
of prescribed guidelines”.
6. Amendment of section 20.
The Act is amended in section 20 by replacing paragraph (c) as follows
“(c) providing architectural services for single family dwellings other than
where the multiple production of houses is involved or where the buildings
concerned are to be not more than
(i) two storeys in height; and
(ii) two thousand five hundred (2500) square feet”.
7. Amendment of section 27.
The Act is amended in section 27(c) by replacing the expression “certificates, and other
documents” with the expression “certificates, code of professional conduct and other
documents”.
8. Amendment of Act to insert section 29.
The Act is amended by inserting immediately after section 28 a new section 29 as
follows
“29. Transition
Section 9(c) shall cease to have effect one year after the Board commences the
registration of architects.”.
9. Amendment of Schedule 1.
The Act is amended in Schedule 1 by

3
Architects Registration (Amendment) Act, 2025 – 25.
(a) in paragraph (1)(b)(i), replacing the expression “registered architects”
with the expression “architects who qualify to be registered pursuant to
the provisions of this Act”.
(b) in paragraph (1)(b)(ii), replacing the expression “registered architect”
with the expression “architect who qualifies to be registered pursuant to
the provisions of this Act and who shall be nominated by the Ministry of
Sustainable Development”.
(c) inserting immediately after paragraph 1 subparagraph (2), the following
new subparagraphs (3), (4) and (5)
“ (3) At least one of the members appointed to the Board shall be an
architect whose practice is based in Nevis.
(4) Provided that in the first year after the commencement of the
Act, the Board shall consist of one additional member (that is six members)
who shall be a registered architect pursuant to section 9(c), and whose
term of appointment shall not exceed 1 year.
(5) The member referred to in (1)(b)(iii) who is not a registered
architect shall be an attorney-at-law.”.
LANEIN BLANCHETTE
Speaker
Passed by the National Assembly this 31st day of October, 2025.
TREVLYN STAPLETON
Clerk of the National Assembly
GOVERNMENT PRINTERY
ST. KITTS, W. I.
